mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 1.55% in Nov 2019 reflects recent rate cuts
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate above a 0.3 percentage point monthly increase signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.5% in Nov 2019 near cycle lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market tightening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite UNRATE spike, or correlation between UNRATE changes and 60-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint contraction in retail sales and housing starts signals weakening aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue growth expectations. When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline > 2% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 527994M and HOUST at 1314K in Oct-Nov 2019
regime_hint: demand-side slowdown with synchronized housing and consumption weakness
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS decline × HOUST decline) produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or joint declines do not predict negative returns at higher frequency than either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 0.5% month-over-month signals robust supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, indicating strong corporate earnings growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: manufacturing expansion with positive supply shocks
failure_condition: INDPRO monthly gains > 0.5% do not predict positive 5-day returns at statistically significant levels (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates below 100K new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market softening that precedes broader economic weakness and negative equity returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change < 100K while UNRATE unchanged
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market deceleration with hidden slack
failure_condition: Joint signal (weak PAYEMS growth with stable UNRATE) fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPI growth below 2% annually combined with federal funds rate above 1.5% signals monetary policy is restrictive relative to inflation, constraining liquidity and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 257.936 and FEDFUNDS at 1.55% in Nov 2019
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy in low-inflation environment
failure_condition: Restrictive policy signal (low inflation + elevated rates) does not produce negative 20-day returns at statistically significant levels (t-test p > 0.05), or interaction term Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured quarterly decelerates below 2% annualized rate, signaling weakening economic momentum that reduces corporate profit growth expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Sub-2% GDP growth does not predict negative 60-day returns at statistically significant levels (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST show sustained month-over-month growth above 1% combined with rising retail sales RSAFS, signaling strong consumer and construction demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days in expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 1% month-over-month and RSAFS rises > 0.5% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1314K and RSAFS at 527994M in Oct-Nov 2019
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with housing and consumption strength
failure_condition: Joint demand strength signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction term produces Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.
